steps.title: Apply the vertical shift
steps.content: The second platform is $2$ feet shorter than the first platform. Since the ball is launched the same way, the entire height graph shifts down by $2$ feet while keeping the same shape.

steps.title: Update the key heights
steps.content: The first graph starts at $7$ feet, so the second graph starts at $7-2=5$ feet. The vertex also shifts down by $2$ feet, from about $(0.3{,}8.3)$ to about $(0.3{,}6.3)$.

steps.title: Choose the shifted graph
steps.content: The matching graph must open downward, start at $(0{,}5)$, have vertex about $(0.3{,}6.3)$, and return to about $(0.6{,}5)$. Choice A is too low, and Choice C and Choice D are too high. The answer is B.
